pages.raw_text:
OBSERVATION-DISCLOSURES


The Seattle republican city con-
vention will be held Feb. 81.


Charles Drury was elected pres
ident of Tacoma school board last
Saturday.


Representatives of Seattle flour
mills say the Chinese boycott is
seriously affecting their business.


The official report of Assayeyr
Wing shows total deposits at the
Seattle assay office of SIG, 006,74S.


Engineers declare Snoqualmie
pass the only feasible route left
open to the Milwaukee road to
reach the coast.


In order to secure the Christian
Endeavor convention in Seattle
next year, a $4,000 pledge must be
given toward defraying expenses.


Frank Brown, manager of the
Jumbo (Hump) mine, has so far
frustrated all attempts to remove
him from the management.


"Kid" White, the Tacoma boy
who was recently convicted of the
murder in Seattle, will be hanged
Jan. &c. The death warrant was
signed Saturday.


A Northern Pacific local train,
south bound from Seattle to Olym-
pia, collided in the fog with a light
engine in South Tacoma yesterday.
No one was seriously hurt.


In the presence of Ter husband,
who had come to Portland from st.
Paul, Mimi, to induce her to aban-
don her mode of life, Mrs. Eva Toy
committed suicide by taking car
bolic acid early yesterday.


County Attorney Bates of Pierce
county has refused to aid the grand
jury at Tacoma by appointing an
attorney to work with the jury in
the investigation of municipal af
fairs now pending in Tacoma.


John G. Brady, governor Of AL
aska, will resign his Office to ac-
cept the presidency of the Rey-
nolds-Alaska Development COM
pany, the same company over
which he had such strenuous
time with President Theodore
Roosevelt.


The Elks' committee of arrange
mentS for the dedication of the
temple at Moscow, Idaho, on Jan
II, has secured special rates of a
fare and a third for round trips on
the Northern Pacific and the o. r.
& N.


The meeting of the republican
county central committee at Walla
Walla Saturday afternoon called
for the purpose of ousting Eugene
Lorton from the Craftsmanship of
the committee, failed to carry out
the plan. Instead of ousting Lor-
ton, the committee endorsed him.


Bob Shadwell, a notorious dope"
fiend and desperate of Butte, was
found dead, with his boots on, in
the Chinese section at Butte yes
terday. Shadwell was once con-
victed of murder and sentenced to
